what_it_does: Acceptance test for the configurable interactive inbox (B3 / §6B QUEUE mode). It creates a real FsStore and Suite, builds a small graph, defers an interactive build offer, verifies it persists as a deferred_offer surfaced item, starts a fresh Suite to read it back, revives the proposal unchanged, proves that defer/revive never dispatch the offered verb, resolves the item to clear it from live escalations, checks fail-loud error cases, and verifies a single-option (B1) offer also defers and revives.
observations: Uses a real FsStore in a temporary directory plus real node discovery, not a mocked model.
Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1 so surfaced items are tagged test_origin and the test reads raw store/inbox lists to bypass hygiene filtering.
Monkey-patches suite._dispatch_rhm_action to record every dispatched action and enforce the consent gate.
The deferred offer payload preserves verb, address, args, interactive flag, direction flag, options list, and operator note.
fresh_suite() creates a brand new Suite over the existing store directory to simulate a process restart.
Resolving the surfaced item with resolve_surfaced(sid, 'approve', ...) removes it from live_escalations.
Fail-loud checks cover non-dict proposals, proposals lacking verb/options, missing ids, and reviving non-deferred items.
Includes a B1 single-option offer case to confirm the surface mapping is generic.
standouts: Direct spy on the RHM dispatcher to prove defer/revive execute nothing.
Fresh Suite round-trip proves persistence across simulated process restart.
Wrong-card revive guard: reviving a review item raises ValueError.
B1/B2 generic defer/revive coverage in one acceptance script.
conventions: Heavy docstring maps each test section to a named completion criterion (B3).
Global PASS counter with a local check() helper for assertions and progress output.
tempfile.mkdtemp with shutil.rmtree cleanup in a try/finally block.
Manual sys.path insertion and COMPANY_TEST_RUN environment setup at the top.
Direct store/inbox access for assertions rather than only public API.
concerns: Monkey-patches a private method (_dispatch_rhm_action), coupling the test to internal dispatch plumbing.
raises() helper silently returns False for unexpected exception types, which could hide real bugs during fail-loud checks.
Global PASS counter is not thread-safe and mixes all scenarios into one sequential script.
Raw store schema assertions (action == 'deferred_offer', resolved == None) may break if storage representation changes.
Graph creation (constant + uppercase nodes) is performed even though the inbox semantics under test do not obviously depend on it.
notes: An end-to-end acceptance script that treats the inbox as a single persisted queue and proves deferred interactive offers stay alive, revivable, and unexecuted until an explicit approve.
questions: Where exactly are defer_offer, revive_offer, and resolve_surfaced implemented (Suite methods or elsewhere)?
What is the full schema expected for a deferred_offer payload, and is it enforced by the implementation or only by this test?
How does COMPANY_TEST_RUN tagging interact with inbox_lanes filtering in production code?
Is _dispatch_rhm_action the canonical hook for all RHM verb execution, or are there other dispatch paths that could bypass the spy?
Why is graph construction required before exercising inbox defer/revive?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in the docstring (B3 configurable interactive inbox: persist, revive, no dispatch until approve, fail loud) matches the assertions and scenarios in the file.
apparent_themes: Consent gate / nothing runs until approved
Single source of truth inbox store (no parallel queue)
intention_signals: The file is trying to demonstrate that interactive offers can be parked in the inbox as genuine, long-lived conversations that survive restarts and can be reopened safely, while strictly preventing accidental execution during defer or revive.
novelty: It simulates a process restart by constructing a fresh Suite over the same store directory and uses a dispatched-action spy to prove the consent gate, combining B1/B2 surface semantics with queue persistence in one acceptance test.
contribution: An acceptance harness that defines the expected lifecycle of a deferred interactive offer (persist, revive, resolve, fail-loud) and the invariant that defer/revive never dispatch the offered verb.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test that exercises the interactive inbox queue by deferring and reviving real surfaced offers across a simulated process restart, verifying persistence, no premature dispatch, fail-loud errors, and resolution cleanup.
intention_for_embedding: To prove B3 completion criteria: deferred interactive offers remain live, revivable, unexecuted until approval, and correctly cleared after resolution.

symbols.does: Local assertion helper that increments a global PASS counter and prints an ok message.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) asserts the supplied condition, increments the module-level PASS counter on success, and prints a formatted ok line; on failure it raises with the label.

symbols.does: Creates a new Suite over an existing store directory to simulate a process restart.
symbols.description: fresh_suite(store_root) instantiates a fresh FsStore, discovers nodes into a new NodeRegistry, and returns a new Suite (plus the store) reading the already-persisted state.

symbols.does: Records every RHM action dispatched by the Suite during defer/revive.
symbols.description: A closure assigned to suite._dispatch_rhm_action that appends each action to a dispatched list before delegating to the original dispatch method, used to prove the consent gate is not violated.

symbols.does: Helper that returns True only if a callable raises the expected exception type.
symbols.description: raises(fn, exc) runs the given callable and returns True if it raises exactly the expected exception; it returns False for any other exception or for no exception, which is used to assert fail-loud behavior.
